pass_criteria: True sae-spelling absorption rate measured for at least 3 configs. Sigmoid vs. linear BIC comparison computed.

findings.sigmoid_fit: LRT p=0.027 is borderline with n=5 cross-layer data points. BIC delta=2.42 favors sigmoid over linear. Nominal inflection at L0_c=81. Bootstrap CI is uninformative (width: 1 to 790M). Should be treated as directional support only.
findings.ajt_inverse_eda: All 3 AJT variants show NEGATIVE EDA_delta (letter EDA < nonletter EDA). AJT mean letter EDA=0.502 vs jb L6 letter EDA=0.681. This inversion is architecture-specific and present across all AJT L0 values.
findings.width_null_result: Width scaling (L8, widths 12k-98k at matched L0~50): EDA_delta DECREASES with width (rho=-1.0, 3-point). Opposite to theory prediction that wider SAEs show more absorption.
findings.true_absorption_proxy: Absorption rates using split-feature non-firing proxy: L2=10%, L6=49%, L10=51%. Note: these use only 10 words per letter and letter-selective feature identification — rates may be noisy. The increase from L2 to L6/L10 is consistent with EDA AUROC improvement across layers.

caveats: Cross-layer analysis confounds L0 with representation maturity.
Bootstrap CI for L0_c is meaningless with n=5.
True absorption rates use a proxy method (not exact Chanin et al. labels).
AJT inverse EDA unexplained without true absorption rate for AJT.
Width analysis: the 3-point perfect Spearman rho=-1.0 is over-determined (any monotone relationship gives rho=-1 or +1 with 3 points).
recommended_follow_up: E.1: Within-layer sparsity sweep using AJT suite (3 L0 values at fixed layer/width) for cleaner phase transition evidence.
B.3: Compare true absorption rates (sae-spelling) for jb vs AJT SAEs at matched L0.
Use 100 tokens/letter (not 10 words) for more reliable absorption rate estimates.
